feature: hex chat — Full AI REPL (context, slash commands, tool use, sessions)
description: Elevates hex chat from a streaming chat TUI into a full AI REPL: automatic project context injection (the model knows about ADRs, workplans, agents), slash commands (/help /clear /model /adr /plan /save), tool use loop (model can call hex operations), and session persistence with --resume.

phases.name: Project context injection
phases.description: Fetch hex project state on chat startup and inject as a system prompt so the model knows about the current project without the user explaining it.

tasks.name: Build context prompt from nexus APIs and inject as system message
tasks.description:
In `hex-cli/src/tui/chat.rs`, add an async function `fetch_project_context(nexus: &NexusClient) -> String` that:
1. Calls `nexus.get("/api/status")` — extracts project name, project_id, nexus version
2. Calls `nexus.get("/api/workplans")` (or `/api/hexflo/swarms`) — lists active swarms/workplans with task counts
3. Calls `nexus.get("/api/adrs")` — gets up to 10 most recent ADRs (title, status)
4. Calls `nexus.get("/api/inference/providers")` or `/api/inference/list` — gets registered inference providers
5. Builds a system prompt string:
```
You are an AI assistant embedded in the hex development environment.

Project: {name} ({id})
Active workplans: {list or 'none'}
Recent ADRs: {titles + status}
Inference providers: {list}

You can help with: architecture decisions, ADR research, workplan status, code analysis, and general development questions. The user may type /help to see available commands.
```
In `ChatApp::new()`, store `context_system: Option<String>`. In `run()`, call `fetch_project_context(&nexus).await` before starting the TUI, store in app. In `send_message()`, merge `context_system` + `self.system` into the `system` field sent to the API (context first, user system appended with a newline if both exist).

Handle errors gracefully — if context fetch fails, skip it silently (don't block chat startup).

Also add `--no-context` flag to `ChatArgs` (bool, default false) that skips context injection.

`cargo check -p hex-cli` must pass. Commit: `git add hex-cli/src/tui/chat.rs hex-cli/src/commands/chat.rs && git commit -m 'feat(hex-chat): inject project context as system prompt on startup'`

phases.name: Slash command parser and built-in skills
phases.description: Parse /command input before sending to the model. Execute slash commands client-side and display results inline. No inference request is made for slash commands.

tasks.name: Create tui/skills.rs with slash command parser and handlers
tasks.description:
Create `hex-cli/src/tui/skills.rs` with:

```rust
pub enum SkillResult {
    Lines(Vec<String>),   // display these lines inline as a 'system' message
    ClearHistory,
    SwitchModel(String),
    Noop,
    Unknown(String),
}

pub fn is_slash_command(input: &str) -> bool {
    input.trim_start().starts_with('/')
}

pub async fn dispatch(input: &str, nexus_url: &str) -> SkillResult { ... }
```

Handle these commands in `dispatch()`:
- `/help` → return Lines with table of all slash commands + descriptions
- `/clear` → return ClearHistory
- `/model <name>` → return SwitchModel(name)
- `/context` → call GET /api/status, return Lines showing current context
- `/adr <query>` → call GET /api/adrs/search?q=<query> (or /api/adrs with filter), format results as Lines
- `/plan` → call GET /api/workplans or /api/hexflo/swarms, format as Lines with task counts
- `/save` → return Lines with a 'session saved' message (actual save happens separately)
- `/<anything-else>` → return Unknown(cmd)

For API calls in skills, use `reqwest::Client::new()` since we don't have NexusClient here (we only have the URL). Simple GET with JSON response.

Register in `tui/mod.rs`: `pub mod skills;`

In `tui/chat.rs`, update `send_message()` or the Enter key handler:
```rust
if crate::tui::skills::is_slash_command(&app.input) {
    let result = crate::tui::skills::dispatch(&app.input, &app.nexus_url).await;
    // handle result: push system message, clear history, switch model, etc.
    app.input.clear();
    return; // don't send to inference
}
```

For slash command results, add a new `Role::System` variant to render them in a distinct color (dim italic, no role label, just the content lines in DarkGray).

`cargo check -p hex-cli` must pass. Commit: `git add hex-cli/src/tui/skills.rs hex-cli/src/tui/mod.rs hex-cli/src/tui/chat.rs && git commit -m 'feat(hex-chat): slash command parser and built-in skills (/help /clear /model /adr /plan)'`

phases.name: Session persistence — auto-save and --resume
phases.description: Auto-save conversations to ~/.hex/sessions/ after each turn. Add hex chat --resume for session picker.

tasks.name: Create tui/session.rs for save/load + wire --resume into chat command
tasks.description:
Create `hex-cli/src/tui/session.rs`:

```rust
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ChatSession {
    pub id: String,           // uuid v4
    pub created_at: String,   // ISO 8601
    pub updated_at: String,
    pub model: String,
    pub project_id: String,
    pub messages: Vec<SessionMessage>,
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct SessionMessage {
    pub role: String,   // "user" | "assistant" | "system"
    pub content: String,
}

impl ChatSession {
    pub fn new(model: &str, project_id: &str) -> Self { ... } // generates uuid, timestamps
    pub fn save(&self) -> anyhow::Result<()> { ... }  // writes to ~/.hex/sessions/chat-{id}.json
    pub fn load(id: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Self> { ... }
    pub fn list_recent(limit: usize) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<ChatSession>> { ... } // sorted by updated_at
}

pub fn sessions_dir() -> std::path::PathBuf {
    dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_default().join(".hex").join("sessions")
}
```

In `tui/chat.rs`:
- Add `session: ChatSession` to `ChatApp`
- After `StreamEvent::Done`, call `app.session.messages.push(...)` for both user + assistant messages, then `app.session.save()?` (ignore error, don't block)
- `app.session.model` updates with each Done event

In `commands/chat.rs`:
- Add `--resume` flag to `ChatArgs` (optional String, short -r)
- If `--resume` is provided with a UUID: load session, restore `app.messages` from it, skip context fetch
- If `--resume` is provided without a value (or `--resume-pick`): show a crossterm-based arrow-key picker of recent sessions (no ratatui needed — simple terminal list with ↑↓ + Enter); restore selected session

Add `dirs = "5"` to hex-cli/Cargo.toml for home_dir().
Register `pub mod session` in `tui/mod.rs`.
`cargo check -p hex-cli` must pass. Commit appropriately.

phases.name: Integration build + full smoke test
phases.description: Full release build, verify context injection works, slash commands respond correctly, sessions save.

tasks.name: Release build + smoke tests
tasks.description:
Run `cargo build -p hex-cli --release`. Test:
1. `hex chat --no-tui --message 'what is the active workplan?'` — verify response references actual project context
2. `hex chat --no-context --no-tui --message 'hello'` — verify --no-context flag works
Fix any compile issues. Final commit: `git add -u && git commit -m 'feat(hex-chat-repl): context injection, slash commands, session persistence'`
